title: Gap Fill Opening-Auction Doctrine

summary.thesis: The gap is an opening-auction context anchor. What matters is not only whether it fills, but whether price is accepted or rejected after entering the gap and prior value.
summary.operator_translation: Do not treat a gap as a blind target or blind reversal trigger. Watch how the auction behaves as it approaches, fills, and either accepts or rejects the old value.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card to frame replay analysis around gap context, fill attempts, and post-fill acceptance or rejection.

when_relevant: The session opened with a meaningful RTH gap relative to prior close, range, or value.
Price is approaching the gap edge or already interacting with it.
The opening auction is still resolving whether old value will be reaccepted.
required_evidence: first touch, partial fill, or full fill can be identified
post-fill acceptance or rejection behavior can be described
confirmation_signals: Price is accepted back into the gap and prior value.
Price fills the gap and holds rather than instantly rejecting.
The opening auction tempo supports re-acceptance of old value.
invalidation_signals: Gap is only touched and strongly rejected.
Partial fill fails and the market resumes the original imbalance away from the gap.
Price cannot hold inside prior value after the fill.
no_trade_conditions: Do not assume every gap should fill.
Do not trade just because the gap is almost filled if acceptance is still unclear.
Do not ignore prior value placement and opening tempo when judging the gap script.

entry_archetypes.name: accepted fill continuation
entry_archetypes.description: Trade in the direction of acceptance only after price is actually held inside the repaired area.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Skip if the fill is only a brief touch and rejection starts immediately.

entry_archetypes.name: failed fill reversal
entry_archetypes.description: Fade the fill only after rejection from the gap area is clear and the market reasserts the original imbalance.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Skip if price is still negotiating inside prior value.

management_notes: Gap fill is context, not a blind setup.
Treat full fill and accepted fill as different states.
Partial fills carry information and should be measured, not hand-waved.
review_questions: Was the market actually accepted back into the gap or only tagged it?
Did the operator confuse full fill with re-acceptance?
Was the opening tempo compatible with a fill-and-hold or fill-and-reject script?
Where should the operator have stayed out because the gap context was unresolved?
